Share your details below. Our team will review and reach out within 2 working days — no commitment required.

By checking this, you agree to enter into a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Zero's School to set up and run a fully managed SIC. No fees. No obligations beyond providing student participation.

✓

We've received your details.

Our team will reach out within 2 working days to walk you through the onboarding process.

How Zero's School runs your SIC — end to end.

From government registration to the student funding pitch — here is every step Zero's School handles on your behalf.

Phase 1 — Setup
1

School Onboarding

We formally onboard your school and brief your administration on the SIC mandate, timeline, and what to expect throughout the year.

2

Government Portal Registration

We register your school on the relevant government portals, ensuring full compliance with the SIC directive from day one.

3

Form the Innovation Panel

We help constitute your SIC panel — comprising the Principal, designated co-ordinators, teachers, and the student council representatives.

4

Train the Panel

Each panel member is briefed on their specific responsibilities — what they are expected to do before, during, and after every session.

Phase 2 — Student Activation
5

Student Onboarding

Students are registered on the Zero's School portal and introduced to the program — what innovation means, and why it matters for their future.

6

Ambition Mapping

Students go through a structured exercise to identify their interests, strengths, and areas of curiosity — which shapes how they engage with sessions.

7

Finalise Session Slots with Counsellor

Zero's School coordinates with your school counsellor to lock in 10 session slots across the academic calendar — minimal disruption, maximum impact.

Phase 3 — The 10 Annual Sessions
8

Each Session: 30-Min Expert Presentation

A knowledge partner — university professor, startup founder, or industry expert — delivers a focused 30-minute session on innovation, technology, or entrepreneurship.

9

Student Study Material on the Portal

After each session, curated material is published on the Zero's School portal. Students study at their own pace and submit innovative ideas based on what they've learnt.

10

Evaluation by Teachers & Mentors

Submitted ideas are reviewed by teachers and Zero's School mentors, who provide structured feedback to help students sharpen their thinking.

Phase 4 — Recognition & Impact
11

Top Ideas Pitch to a Broader Panel

The strongest ideas are shortlisted. Students present to a panel of mentors, school leadership, and peers in a structured pitch session.

12

Best Idea Gets a Funding Pitch Opportunity

The winning idea earns an opportunity to pitch to a broader network for potential funding and real-world development support.

13

Government Compliance — Fully Handled

All session records, participation data, ideas submitted, and outcomes are uploaded to the government portal. Your school stays compliant, automatically.

School Innovation Councils · Government of India Initiative

The Government wants your school
to have an SIC.
We'll build it for you.

School Innovation Councils are a government directive under NEP 2020. Most schools don't know where to start. Zero's School sets up and runs the entire SIC for your school — 10 sessions a year, industry and university experts, and students who are genuinely better prepared for the future. Your cost: zero.

The Challenge

The mandate exists.
The infrastructure doesn't.

The Government of India has directed schools to set up School Innovation Councils — to build critical thinking, problem-solving, and entrepreneurial mindsets among students.

Most schools want to act on this. But running a structured SIC requires resources, networks, and time that most schools simply don't have in place.

  • No dedicated staff to coordinate innovation programming
  • No access to mentors, founders, or professors who will reliably show up
  • No curriculum or structure to make sessions meaningful for students
  • No clarity on what government compliance actually requires

"Schools are expected to build an innovation culture overnight — without the tools, the people, or the playbook to do it."

What We Do

We set it up.
We run it.
You lead.

Zero's School takes complete ownership of your School Innovation Council — from government registration to annual compliance. Your school provides the students. We handle everything else.

Full implementation.
Zero burden.

You get a live, government-compliant School Innovation Council — without adding workload to your staff or spending a rupee.

Cost to your school: ₹0

  • ✓
    10 expert-led sessions every year

  • ✓
    Government compliance, handled
    We register your school and upload all required documentation to government portals.
  • ✓
    Real exposure for your students
    Students interact directly with founders, professors, and technology leaders.
  • ✓
    Best ideas get funding opportunities
    Top student ideas are pitched to a broader panel and can earn access to real funding.
Our Ecosystem

The people who show up for your students.

Zero's School's sessions are delivered by a curated network of practitioners — people who have built things, led institutions, and are invested in the next generation.

University Professors

Academic faculty who connect students to deeper fields of knowledge and inspire curiosity about higher education.

Startup Founders

Practitioners who share the reality of building a venture — the decisions, the pivots, and what they'd do differently.

Industry Mentors

Senior professionals from tech, manufacturing, and services bringing real-world perspective into the classroom.

Technology Leaders

Experts in AI, deep tech, and emerging fields who help students see what the careers of the future look like.
